title: IPaC boundary: information production, not generic document management
description: IPaC is bounded around production, preservation, validation, circulation and evolution of living knowledge. It is not document workflow, content publishing or task management.
stable_evidence: Need/Concept artifacts repeatedly distinguish living knowledge circulation from document workflow.
not_to_break: Do not reduce IPaC to document management, content operations, marketing workflow or task tracking.

title: Living knowledge boundary
description: The core object is living knowledge: knowledge with context, use, feedback, review, canon update potential and trace.
stable_evidence: Knowledge Clump and Living Knowledge repeatedly serve as core concepts.
not_to_break: Do not treat files as the primary object and knowledge as a byproduct.

title: Architecture vs execution boundary
description: Stable internal invariants must be separated from temporary execution choices.
stable_evidence: User explicitly corrected Phase 4: architecture invariants, not execution plans, MVPs or publication mechanics.
not_to_break: Do not canonize execution mechanisms as architectural core.
execution_candidate_note: Specific channels, dashboards, publication waves and launch plans are execution candidates.

title: Human-AI collaboration boundary
description: AI is included as methodological guide and continuity layer; the human remains the subject carrying intent, authorship and final judgement.
stable_evidence: AI Mentor Layer established this boundary.
not_to_break: Do not transfer authorship or final strategic judgement from human to AI.

title: Human Subject
description: Human Subject carries living intention, intuition, direction, judgement, authorship and the final 'то / не то' evaluation.
stable_evidence: Intersubjectivity and AI Mentor Layer repeatedly define this role.
not_to_break: Do not remove human subjecthood from meaning and strategic decisions.

title: AI Mentor / Methodological Guide
description: AI Mentor holds frameworks, formal schemes, boot/handoff/recovery discipline and helps convert living intent into structured artifacts.
stable_evidence: AI Mentor Layer pack and mentor discussion.
not_to_break: Do not let AI Mentor become guru, commander or author-substitute.

title: Knowledge Clump / Знаниевый Сгусток
description: Central knowledge formation: artifacts plus context, history, decisions, links, use, feedback, implicit meaning and emergent potential.
stable_evidence: Defined and reused as the core object of IPaC.
not_to_break: Do not flatten Knowledge Clump into a folder, summary, archive or single document.

title: Knowledge Artifact
description: Explicit projections of the Knowledge Clump: documents, prompts, schemas, registers, status files, maps, diagrams and packages.
stable_evidence: Every phase produces portable artifacts with README/MANIFEST/status/registers/maps.
not_to_break: Do not confuse artifact with the whole Knowledge Clump.

title: Canon
description: Current accepted state of definitions, formulas, decisions, routes, boundaries and stable project knowledge.
stable_evidence: Canon appears as memory spine for continuity, boot, updates and trace.
not_to_break: Do not update canon directly from raw feedback or temporary excitement.

title: Trace
description: Preserved history of how knowledge, decisions, artifacts and status changes emerged, changed and were used.
stable_evidence: Decision records, status files, Git/Obsidian trace and learning patches.
not_to_break: Do not lose decision history, source context or reasons for change.

title: Feedback / Evidence
description: Feedback is raw evidence from use or environment. It is not a conclusion or immediate command to change canon.
stable_evidence: Feedback as Evidence and 'Raw first, interpret later'.
not_to_break: Do not treat likes, silence, comments or reactions as conclusions without review.

title: Projection
description: Outward or inward expression of a Knowledge Clump into a specific form, audience, context or use. Channels are carriers; projection is the invariant.
stable_evidence: Repeated distinction between core clump and channel outputs/artifacts.
not_to_break: Do not make any one projection equal to the core concept.
execution_candidate_note: Specific external channels are execution candidates.

title: Human Subject ↔ AI Mentor
description: Intersubjective collaboration: human gives intent and judgement; AI gives structure, frameworks, trace discipline and artifact generation.
stable_evidence: Intersubjectivity and mentor discussions stabilize this relation.
not_to_break: Do not collapse into command-response or autonomous AI control.

title: Knowledge Clump → Artifact
description: Artifacts are projections, carriers and operational surfaces of the Knowledge Clump, not substitutes for it.
stable_evidence: Thread exports, phase artifacts, prompt packs and diagrams serve as projections.
not_to_break: Do not let artifact production replace knowledge continuity.

title: Feedback → Review → Canon
description: Feedback must be preserved, reviewed and only then potentially routed into canon or next projection decisions.
stable_evidence: Established as raw feedback → evidence/noise separation → review → decision → trace.
not_to_break: Do not update canon from unreviewed feedback.
execution_candidate_note: Specific numbered protocols are execution/governance implementations.

title: Canon ↔ Trace
description: Canon holds current accepted state; trace records how that state came to be and changes.
stable_evidence: Decision records, status files, Git/Obsidian trace and learning patches.
not_to_break: Do not separate accepted knowledge from its change history.

title: Living impulse to structured artifact flow
description: A recurring flow turns living impulse into formula, structure, artifact, status and next route.
stable_evidence: Repeated pattern: impulse → formula → block → package → status.
not_to_break: Do not skip formulation and status fixation.

title: Artifact to bootable context flow
description: Artifacts must be packaged so another Project, plan or agent can recover context: README, MANIFEST, status, registers and primary docs.
stable_evidence: Every major package used this structure; bootability became stable.
not_to_break: Do not create non-portable artifacts without orientation/status layers.

title: Feedback to learning flow
description: Raw feedback flows into evidence preservation, classification, review, decision candidates and trace before becoming learning.
stable_evidence: Feedback discipline repeatedly fixed this flow.
not_to_break: Do not transform reaction directly into learning.

title: Handoff flow between environments
description: Moving between chats, Projects, plans or agents requires current status, decisions, open loops, one next action, not-doing and needed files.
stable_evidence: Handoff Protocol and Mentor Layer established continuity invariant.
not_to_break: Do not start new environments without handoff/boot recovery.

title: No canon update without review
description: Canon must not be changed directly from raw reaction, speculation or temporary execution signals.
stable_evidence: Repeated across Feedback as Evidence and review protocols.
not_to_break: Never let raw feedback become immediate canon.

title: Maturity separation
description: Concepts must be classified by maturity and not prematurely promoted into architecture.
stable_evidence: Phase 2 maturity classification and Phase 3 hypothesis statuses.
not_to_break: Do not treat powerful metaphors as validated architecture.

title: Architecture / execution separation
description: Stable architecture must be separated from execution candidates such as channels, launch plans, dashboards and specific tooling choices.
stable_evidence: User explicitly corrected Phase 4 to architecture invariants only.
not_to_break: Do not smuggle execution planning into architecture artifacts.
execution_candidate_note: Execution candidates can be tracked separately.

title: Evidence-based hypothesis promotion
description: Hypotheses move toward validated status only when evidence is captured and routed through review and trace.
stable_evidence: Phase 3 rule: no status upgrade without evidence.
not_to_break: Do not promote unresolved/speculative ideas based on attractiveness alone.

title: Knowledge lifecycle invariant
description: Stable lifecycle: impulse → formulation → structuring → artifact → canon/trace → projection/use → feedback/evidence → review → decision → updated canon/trace.
stable_evidence: Lifecycle appears in diagrams, Need/Concept artifacts and living knowledge discussions.
not_to_break: Do not remove feedback, review or trace from the lifecycle.

title: Portability invariant
description: Knowledge must be portable across contexts without losing status, origin, structure or next route.
stable_evidence: ZIP packages, boot protocols and portation work.
not_to_break: Do not create artifacts understandable only inside one chat context.

title: Rebuild invariant
description: Living knowledge must be able to rebuild itself based on reviewed evidence and trace while preserving continuity.
stable_evidence: Controlled rebuild, learning patches, canon updates and trace repeatedly appear.
not_to_break: Do not rebuild without trace or continuity.

title: Human owns intent and strategic judgement
description: Human owns living intention, authorship, strategic choice, acceptance/rejection and judgement of whether the result is 'то / не то'.
stable_evidence: Consistent across intersubjectivity and AI Mentor Layer.
not_to_break: Do not make AI owner of intention.

title: AI owns methodological support, not authorship
description: AI provides methods, structure, frameworks, comparison, risk detection, formatting, status discipline and operational packaging.
stable_evidence: AI Mentor defined as methodological guide and continuity layer.
not_to_break: Do not present AI guidance as final authority or project authorship.

title: Do not break living meaning into dead documentation
description: Formalization must preserve living core, context, trace and capacity for further development.
stable_evidence: Central tension: formalize without killing living meaning.
not_to_break: Do not over-bureaucratize into lifeless templates.

title: Do not break bootability
description: Any project state must remain recoverable in a new context through instructions, sources, status and handoff.
stable_evidence: Portation phase and Mentor Layer make bootability central.
not_to_break: Do not leave important state only in implicit conversation memory.

title: Do not break architecture/execution distinction
description: Temporary implementation choices must not be mistaken for stable architecture.
stable_evidence: User’s correction to Phase 4 directly requires this distinction.
not_to_break: Do not include MVP/channel/publication mechanics in architectural core.

title: Specific publication channels are not architecture
description: Named publication channels and channel sequences are execution candidates, not architecture invariants. The invariant is projection/use/feedback.
stable_evidence: User explicitly instructed not to discuss publication channels unless invariant.
not_to_break: Do not make any specific channel part of core architecture.

title: Dashboards, launch runbooks and MVP plans are not architecture
description: Dashboards, launch runbooks and MVP/MVI plans support execution, but are not core architecture unless abstracted into governance/feedback-flow invariants.
stable_evidence: User explicitly requested no execution plan, MVP or publication mechanics.
not_to_break: Do not include operational dashboards or launch sequences as architecture.

title: Specific numbered protocols are implementation forms, not the invariant itself
description: Numbered protocols and document IDs are useful forms, but the invariant is the role: review, decision, trace, boot, handoff, recovery.
stable_evidence: Many numbered artifacts were created; user asks to extract stable invariants rather than execution artifacts.
not_to_break: Do not confuse artifact numbering with architecture.
execution_candidate_note: Specific IDs are execution/canon references, not architecture by themselves.
